task_completion.description: Verify that the USLCI database is available and active in openLCA.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'uslci' (or similar) listed in the Navigation panel on the left side of the openLCA window.

task_completion.description: Verify that an electricity generation process is located to be used as the reference process.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a process with 'electricity' in its name being selected, right-clicked, or opened in the main editor area.

task_completion.description: Verify that a product system has been created from the electricity process.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a product system tab open in the main editor area. The product system's name should contain 'electricity'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Model Graph tab is opened for the created product system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Model Graph' tab being selected at the bottom of the product system editor view.

task_completion.description: Verify that the product system's model graph includes its connected processes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a node-and-edge graph in the Model Graph tab showing the main electricity process node connected to at least 2 other upstream/linked process nodes.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the openLCA GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard openLCA windows, menus, dialogs, and navigation panels being interacted with.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass GUI interactions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show scripts or commands directly modifying openLCA databases or creating product systems.

integrity.description: Verify that the model graph is genuinely rendered by openLCA.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard openLCA node styles, expand/collapse (+/-) buttons on nodes, and standard routing lines, rather than a static image or manually drawn diagram.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target process must be an electricity-related process from the USLCI database. The resulting product system name should inherit the process name and contain 'electricity'. The model graph must show the reference process connected to at least 2 linked processes (e.g., fuel sources, transmission infrastructure) to satisfy the 'connected processes' requirement.
